Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Effect of building direction on the microstructure and tensile properties of Ti-48Al-2Cr-2Nb alloy additively manufactured by electron beam melting

Measured_Composition.Measurement_Method: Not specified (from EBM specimen composition table)

Composition_Info.Note: Al loss of approximately 2 at% during EBM due to evaporation. Balance Ti computed as 100 - sum(explicit elements).

Process_Text.original: Electron beam melting (EBM) using Arcam A2X system operated at 60 kV acceleration voltage. Layer thickness 90 um per powder layer. Pre-heating temperature 1060 C. Electron beam scanned in zigzag pattern with 90 degree rotation between layers. Building direction angle theta = 0 degrees (loading axis parallel to building direction).
Process_Text.simplified: EBM with 60 kV acceleration voltage, 90 um layer thickness, 1060 C pre-heating, zigzag beam pattern with 90 deg rotation per layer, theta=0 deg.

Microstructure_Text.original: Unique layered microstructure composed of alternating duplex-like regions and gamma bands perpendicular to building direction. Duplex-like region (approximately 60 um thick) predominantly contains lamellar-structured gamma/alpha2 fine grains and fine equiaxed gamma grains. Gamma band region (approximately 30 um thick) composed of coarser equiaxed gamma phase grains forming chains perpendicular to building direction. Alpha2 phase hardly observed in gamma bands.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Alternating duplex-like regions (fine lamellar gamma/alpha2 + fine equiaxed gamma) and gamma bands (coarser equiaxed gamma) perpendicular to build direction.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=0 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Highest yield strength at room temperature among tested orientations

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 800 C; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=0 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=0 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Lowest elongation at room temperature among tested orientations due to layer interfaces perpendicular to loading axis

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 800 C; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=0 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm

Composition_Info.Note: Same alloy as theta=0 deg specimen. Al loss approximately 2 at% expected during EBM.

Process_Text.original: Electron beam melting (EBM) using Arcam A2X system operated at 60 kV acceleration voltage. Layer thickness 90 um per powder layer. Pre-heating temperature 1060 C. Electron beam scanned in zigzag pattern with 90 degree rotation between layers. Building direction angle theta = 45 degrees (loading axis at 45 deg to building direction).
Process_Text.simplified: EBM with 60 kV acceleration voltage, 90 um layer thickness, 1060 C pre-heating, zigzag beam pattern with 90 deg rotation per layer, theta=45 deg.

Microstructure_Text.original: Unique layered microstructure composed of alternating duplex-like regions and gamma bands perpendicular to building direction. Duplex-like region (approximately 60 um thick) predominantly contains lamellar-structured gamma/alpha2 fine grains and fine equiaxed gamma grains. Gamma band region (approximately 30 um thick) composed of coarser equiaxed gamma phase grains. Alpha2 phase hardly observed in gamma bands. At theta=45 deg, layer interfaces oriented at 45 deg to loading direction.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Alternating duplex-like regions and gamma bands perpendicular to build direction; at theta=45 deg, layer interfaces at 45 deg to loading axis enabling shear deformation in gamma bands.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=45 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Slightly lower than theta=0 and 90 deg at room temperature

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 800 C; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=45 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=45 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Surprisingly larger than 2%; highest ductility at room temperature among tested orientations

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 800 C; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=45 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm

Composition_Info.Note: Same alloy as theta=0 deg specimen. Al loss approximately 2 at% expected during EBM.

Process_Text.original: Electron beam melting (EBM) using Arcam A2X system operated at 60 kV acceleration voltage. Layer thickness 90 um per powder layer. Pre-heating temperature 1060 C. Electron beam scanned in zigzag pattern with 90 degree rotation between layers. Building direction angle theta = 90 degrees (loading axis perpendicular to building direction).
Process_Text.simplified: EBM with 60 kV acceleration voltage, 90 um layer thickness, 1060 C pre-heating, zigzag beam pattern with 90 deg rotation per layer, theta=90 deg.

Microstructure_Text.original: Unique layered microstructure composed of alternating duplex-like regions and gamma bands perpendicular to building direction. Duplex-like region (approximately 60 um thick) predominantly contains lamellar-structured gamma/alpha2 fine grains and fine equiaxed gamma grains. Gamma band region (approximately 30 um thick) composed of coarser equiaxed gamma phase grains. At theta=90 deg, loading axis perpendicular to layer interfaces.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Alternating duplex-like regions and gamma bands perpendicular to build direction; at theta=90 deg, loading axis perpendicular to layer interfaces.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=90 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Similar to theta=0 deg; over 550 MPa at room temperature

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 800 C; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=90 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Over 350 MPa at 800 C; BDTT shifted to lower temperature than 0 and 45 deg

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=90 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm
Properties_Info.Note: Low elongation at room temperature; slightly higher than theta=0 deg due to different crack propagation path

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 800 C; nominal strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1; theta=90 deg
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Gauge length 5 mm, cross-section 0.8 x 1.5 mm

Composition_Info.Note: Balance Ti computed as 100 - sum(explicit elements). Spherical particles with average diameter approximately 100 um.

Process_Text.original: Precursor powder for EBM fabrication. Spherical particles with average diameter around 100 um.
Process_Text.simplified: EBM precursor powder, spherical particles, approximately 100 um average diameter.

Microstructure_Text.original: Spherical powder particles used as feedstock for EBM.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Spherical powder feedstock for EBM process.
